About the Rules Foundation

Open infrastructure for encoded law. We build machine-readable encodings of statutes, regulations, and policy rules to serve as ground truth for AI systems.

Our mission

Rules Foundation builds open infrastructure for encoded law. Our mission is to make legal rules machine-readable, verifiable, and accessible to everyone. We're a fiscally sponsored project of the PSL Foundation.

What we do

Atlas

Archive of legal source documents — 53 USC titles, 570+ IRS guidance documents, and 48 states. The foundation for all encoding work.

RAC

Rules as Code DSL for encoding statutes with citations, temporal versioning, and tests. Purpose-built for legal encoding.

AutoRAC

AI-powered encoding pipeline with 3-tier validation. Automated statute encoding with CI testing, oracle validation, and LLM review.

Team

The Rules Foundation is led by Max Ghenis, who also founded PolicyEngine. We're an open-source community project and welcome contributors from all backgrounds — developers, policy experts, legal researchers, and anyone passionate about making rules more transparent and accessible.

Join us on github.com/RulesFoundation

Contact

Have questions or want to get involved? We'd love to hear from you.

hello@rules.foundation

Open infrastructure for encoded law.
